meta.name: MSI-BPD McLean Screening Instrument
meta.fullName: McLean Screening Instrument for Borderline Personality Disorder
meta.description: The MSI-BPD is a brief 10-item screening tool designed to assess the core features of Borderline Personality Disorder (BPD). Based on DSM-IV BPD diagnostic criteria, it uses a cutoff score of 7 for screening and demonstrates good sensitivity and specificity.
meta.author: Zanarini, Vujanovic, Parachini, Boulanger, Frankenburg & Hennen (2003)
meta.reference: Zanarini MC, Vujanovic AA, Parachini EA, Boulanger JL, Frankenburg FR, Hennen J. A screening measure for BPD: the McLean Screening Instrument for Borderline Personality Disorder (MSI-BPD). J Pers Disord. 2003;17(6):568-573.
meta.instruction: The following questions describe experiences you may have had. Please answer Yes or No.

questions.text: Have you often been concerned about being abandoned in relationships and tried desperately to avoid it
Have you had at least two intense and unstable close relationships
Have you been confused about your identity or unsure of who you really are
Have you impulsively done at least two things that could have harmed you (e.g., binge eating, substance misuse, reckless driving, spending sprees, risky sex)
Have you repeatedly had suicidal thoughts or engaged in self-harm
Do your emotions change very quickly (usually lasting a few hours, rarely more than a few days)
Do you often have difficulty controlling your anger or have frequent outbursts
Under stress, do you temporarily become suspicious of others or feel disconnected from reality
Have you shifted between extremely idealizing and extremely devaluing someone

ranges.label: Low Risk
ranges.description: Your score is in the low-risk range and does not indicate significant features of Borderline Personality Disorder.
suggestions: Continue maintaining healthy emotional regulation strategies

ranges.label: Moderate Risk
ranges.description: Your score suggests some features associated with BPD may be present, but it is below the clinical screening threshold.
suggestions: Be mindful of your emotional fluctuations and relationship patterns
If these features affect your daily life, consider seeking a professional assessment

ranges.label: Elevated Risk
ranges.description: Your score is elevated and may indicate several features associated with Borderline Personality Disorder.
suggestions: Consider seeking a professional mental health assessment
Focus on emotion regulation and relationship management
Avoid impulsive behaviors and develop safe coping strategies

ranges.label: High Risk
ranges.description: Your score meets or exceeds the clinical screening threshold (>=7). This suggests possible Borderline Personality Disorder. Please note this is only a screening tool; a diagnosis requires professional clinical assessment.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek evaluation from a psychiatrist or clinical psychologist
Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) is an effective treatment for BPD
If you have thoughts of self-harm or suicide, please contact a crisis helpline immediately
This result does not constitute a diagnosis; do not self-diagnose
